name: Apricots and Campohermosos — circular from Níjar
name_original: Albaricoques y Campohermosos — circular desde Níjar
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Níjar traverse a landscape shaped by its volcanic origin, featuring arid semi-desert hinterland, dramatic rocky cliffs, and pristine sandy beaches. The region includes the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park, offering routes with panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea and protected wetlands. Further inland, cyclists can explore the unique Tabernas Desert with its arid landscapes and canyons, alongside the challenging terrain of the Sierra Alhamilla and Sierra de Los Filabres mountain ranges. Best touring cycling routes around Níjar The most popular touring cycling route is Salinas de Cabo de Gata — circular from San José — Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park, a 25.2 miles (40.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 1 minute to complete. This moderate route offers coastal views and passes through the natural park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Transandalus Trail: Agua Amarga Loop, a moderate 11.8 miles (19.0 km) path. This route provides varied terrain and coastal scenery around Agua Amarga. Local touring cyclists also love the Rodalquilar and Caldera de Majada Redonda Loop, a 16.1 miles (25.9 km) trail leading through volcanic landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 9 minutes. Touring cycling around Níjar is defined by volcanic landscapes, desert hills, and tranquil coastal roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paved surfaces to more difficult unpaved segments. The routes in Níjar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 490 reviews. More than 3400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Níjar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Níjar?
A: There are over 110 touring cycling routes around Níjar, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. These include 17 easy routes, 44 moderate routes, and 51 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for every touring cyclist.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling in Níjar?
A: Níjar's touring cycling routes traverse diverse landscapes, from the volcanic terrain and dramatic rocky cliffs of the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park to the arid canyons of the Tabernas Desert. You'll also find tranquil coastal roads with panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea and routes through mountain ranges like Sierra Alhamilla.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Níjar?
A: Yes, Níjar offers several family-friendly touring cycling routes, particularly those with easier paved surfaces. While specific 'family-friendly' routes aren't explicitly tagged, the 17 easy routes available provide gentler options suitable for families. Look for routes with minimal elevation gain and well-maintained paths.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Níjar?
A: The ideal seasons for touring cycling in Níjar are spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these months, the weather is pleasant and temperatures are moderate, making for comfortable rides. The region's consistent warm climate also makes it suitable for year-round cycling, though summers can be very hot.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Níjar area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Níjar are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1039189417/salinas-de-cabo-de-gata-circular-from-san-jose-cabo-de-gata-nijar-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de Cabo de Gata — circular from San José — Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; offers coastal views and passes through the natural park. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1039226890/rodalquilar-and-caldera-de-majada-redonda-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rodalquilar and Caldera de Majada Redonda Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through volcanic landscapes.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by interesting landmarks. You might cycle past the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2227026" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cortijo del Fraile&lt;/a&gt;, explore the old mining area around Rodalquilar, or see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1060340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Miguel Tower, Cabo de Gata&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041874843/rodalquilar-and-cortijo-del-fraile-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rodalquilar and Cortijo del Fraile Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great way to see some of these historical sites.

Q: Are there any routes that offer coastal views?
A: Absolutely. The Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park is known for its stunning coastline. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041915344/transandalus-trail-agua-amarga-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Transandalus Trail: Agua Amarga Loop&lt;/a&gt; provide varied terrain and beautiful coastal scenery around Agua Amarga. You can also find routes leading to the scenic Cabo de Gata Lighthouse.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Níjar?
A: The touring cycling routes in Níjar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 490 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, stunning coastal views, and the unique volcanic and desert landscapes.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Tabernas Desert?
A: While specific touring cycling routes directly through the heart of the Tabernas Desert are less common for touring bikes, the region's hinterland offers arid, semi-desert landscapes reminiscent of it. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055796448/crater-of-la-granadilla-circular-from-nijar-by-the-via-verde-of-lucainena-de-las-torres" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Crater of la Granadilla - circular from Níjar by the Via Verde of Lucainena de las Torres&lt;/a&gt; route provides a taste of the more inland, rugged terrain.

Q: Can I find routes with significant elevation changes for a more challenging ride?
A: Yes, Níjar offers routes with challenging elevation changes, particularly in areas like the Sierra Alhamilla and Sierra de Los Filabres mountain ranges. There are 51 difficult routes available, providing opportunities for more advanced touring cyclists seeking climbs and descents.

Q: Are there any historical sites or castles along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You might encounter sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/678878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Felipe Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/590244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de los Alumbres&lt;/a&gt;. The area around Rodalquilar also features remnants of its mining past, adding a historical dimension to your ride.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the touring cycling routes?
A: The terrain varies significantly. You'll find easy, paved surfaces suitable for relaxed rides, as well as more difficult unpaved segments, gravel paths, and tracks through volcanic landscapes and desert hills. This diversity allows cyclists to choose routes that match their preferred riding style and bike type.
tips: The town of Los Albaricoques was used as a setting for Western movies, such as "The Good, the Bad and the Ugly" or "Death had a price" by Sergio Leone. Today, you can visit places like the Sad Hill Cemetery, which appears in the movie, or the Fort Bravo film studio.

Los Apricots has been the setting for numerous westerns and the famous trilogy by Sergio Leone. Films like 'Death had a price' or 'For a Fistful of Dollars', with a then young and unknown Clint Eastwood, Lee Van Cleef... have turned this small town into a natural set as well as an economic boost to the area.

Municipality of the province of Almería, within the natural park of Cabo de Gata-Níjar.
The town, located at an altitude of 356 meters, is made up of three main valleys: the Artal, the Hornillo and the Campillo de Gata, and three mountain ranges: the Sierra del Cabo de Gata, the Serrata and the Sierra Alhamilla.
